title: NOC 92022 – Supervisors, furniture and fixtures manufacturing | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92022 are the technical leaders of Canada's diverse furniture and cabinetry manufacturing sector. As a TEER 2 occupation, these professionals oversee the production of everything from high-end residential furniture and office systems to specialized architectural fixtures and kitchen cabinetry. In 2026, as the Canadian construction sector scales up to meet housing demands, these supervisors are critical for managing the high-volume, precision manufacturing required for modern interior fit-outs. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for the OINP Employer Job Offer stream and the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9202 – Supervisors, assembly and fabricationNOC Code92022

duties: Supervision: Coordinate and schedule the activities of workers who assemble, finish, and inspect wood and metal furniture and fixtures.
Operational Strategy: Establish methods to meet strict work schedules and coordinate activities with design, procurement, and shipping departments.
Quality Control: Resolve technical work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and product finish quality.
Resource Management: Requisition materials and supplies; manage equipment maintenance and tool sharpening schedules.
Staff Development: Train workers in job duties, safety procedures (including dust collection and chemical handling), and company policies.
Compliance: Ensure strict adherence to safety rules, environmental regulations regarding VOCs (Volatile Organic Compounds), and ISO quality standards.
Reporting: Prepare detailed production logs, safety audit reports, and material waste summaries for plant management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Work Experience: Several years of experience as a furniture finisher, cabinetmaker, or furniture assembler are mandatory.
Education: A college diploma or vocational training in wood technology, furniture design, or cabinetmaking is often required.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in CAD/CAM software, Lean Manufacturing, and modern finishing techniques.
Strong leadership, spatial reasoning, and the ability to interpret complex blueprints and architectural drawings are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, supervisors in specialized Custom Architectural Woodwork or High-End Office Systems often command a 15–20% premium over the median industrial wage.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable to Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector will face a steady need for new supervisors as 22% of the current workforce approaches retirement by 2030. The primary driver is "Replacement Demand" combined with the industry's shift toward high-efficiency, automated production to compete with global imports.
labour_market_demand: The demand for furniture and fixtures supervisors is Good, particularly in provinces with high residential development. In 2026, Ontario (GTA), Quebec, and British Columbia are reporting a steady need for supervisors to manage the transition from traditional woodworking to CNC-integrated smart manufacturing. With Canada’s "Housing Accelerator Fund" driving record numbers of new condo and townhouse completions, the demand for built-in cabinetry and fixture supervisors remains a top priority for manufacturers.
Advanced Woodworking Technology: Manage "Industry 4.0" shops featuring 5-axis CNC routers, automated edgebanders, and digital nesting software.
Design-Centric Industry: Work in a sector that values both high-tech efficiency and traditional craftsmanship.
Stable Economic Sector: The furniture industry is a multi-billion dollar component of Canadian manufacturing with consistent domestic and export (US) demand.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 2 role is highly prioritized in Express Entry Category-Based Selection for "Trade Occupations."
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Targeted for candidates with experience in "Trade Occupations."
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based furniture or fixture manufacturer.
Manitoba PNP (Skilled Worker Stream): Specifically targets supervisors for the province's extensive furniture manufacturing hub.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors supporting the province’s high-growth residential construction and interior design sectors.
